when_to_use: Use when a continuous metric is sampled at ordered intervals and slope carries meaning
when_not: Avoid when categories have no inherent order or spacing is wildly irregular
accessibility: label the axis with units and add a text summary of the overall direction

when_to_use: Use to compare a single measure across a handful of discrete categories
when_not: Avoid when dozens of bars force tiny widths or scrolling
accessibility: sort bars meaningfully and never rely on hue alone to encode the value

when_to_use: Use to show how one total divides into a few named parts
when_not: Avoid when many slivers appear or parts must be precisely compared
accessibility: print the numeric percentage in each segment label for non-visual readers

when_to_use: Use to reveal the shape spread and skew of one numeric variable
when_not: Avoid when too few points make bins meaningless
accessibility: describe the distribution shape and key statistics in adjacent text

when_to_use: Use to expose how two numeric variables move together
when_not: Avoid when one axis is categorical or points are very few
accessibility: provide a data table fallback since dense points are hard to perceive non-visually

when_to_use: Use to order items high to low so position itself carries meaning
when_not: Avoid when differences between items are negligible
accessibility: order items consistently and announce rank position in labels

when_to_use: Use when values vary across geographic areas and place matters
when_not: Avoid when area size distorts perception of small dense regions
accessibility: pair the map with a sortable table because maps are not screen-reader friendly

when_to_use: Use to trace how quantities move and split between nodes
when_not: Avoid when many crossings turn the diagram into a tangle
accessibility: summarize the largest flows in text since the diagram is purely visual

when_to_use: Use to show nested containment where size encodes a quantity
when_not: Avoid when the structure is deep and leaves become unreadable
accessibility: offer an expandable nested list as an accessible alternative

when_to_use: Use to compare the trajectories of several series over one period
when_not: Avoid when overlapping lines create a spaghetti tangle
accessibility: differentiate series by line style or markers not color alone

when_to_use: Use to spotlight one critical figure that needs immediate attention
when_not: Avoid when context or trend is essential to interpret it
accessibility: give the number a descriptive label and announce the unit explicitly

when_to_use: Use to show actual against a target with qualitative ranges
when_not: Avoid when there is no defined target to measure against
accessibility: state current value and target in text so the comparison is not visual only

when_to_use: Use to show how values fall above or below a meaningful reference
when_not: Avoid when there is no natural zero or baseline point
accessibility: use distinct patterns for positive and negative not just two colors

when_to_use: Use to show a running sum growing across an ordered axis
when_not: Avoid when individual period values matter more than the total
accessibility: clarify in text whether the axis shows cumulative or incremental values

when_to_use: Use to count how often discrete categories occur
when_not: Avoid when categories are continuous and better grouped into bins
accessibility: include raw counts in labels for readers who cannot estimate bar length

when_to_use: Use to reveal connections and clusters among many entities
when_not: Avoid when the graph is dense enough to become a hairball
accessibility: provide an adjacency table because node-link layouts are inaccessible visually

when_to_use: Use to show how a population shrinks across sequential stages
when_not: Avoid when stages are not strictly ordered or counts can rise
accessibility: label each stage with both count and conversion percentage in text

when_to_use: Use to track how groups defined by start period persist over time
when_not: Avoid when cohorts are too small to produce stable rates
accessibility: encode retention with a sequential scale and expose the values in a table

when_to_use: Use to show a daily metric across weeks and months to spot patterns
when_not: Avoid when precise values matter more than relative intensity
accessibility: use a colorblind-safe sequential ramp and label each cell with its value

when_to_use: Use sparingly to show one value within a known min and max range
when_not: Avoid when comparing values or precise reading is required
accessibility: announce the current value and range bounds in text since dials read poorly

when_to_use: Use to show a value across two categorical dimensions at once
when_not: Avoid when the grid is sparse or exact numbers are needed
accessibility: choose a perceptually uniform color scale and provide a value table

when_to_use: Use to show how a band such as min to max evolves over time
when_not: Avoid when only a single representative value is needed
accessibility: describe the band meaning and its bounds in accompanying text

when_to_use: Use to show how the makeup of a total shifts across time
when_not: Avoid when individual series trends matter more than the total
accessibility: order the bands consistently and distinguish them with patterns not color

when_to_use: Use when a scatter has so many points that they overplot badly
when_not: Avoid when few points let individual marks read clearly
accessibility: pair with a sequential legend and offer a summarized data table

when_to_use: Use to scan pairwise relationships across many numeric variables
when_not: Avoid when only one or two pairs of variables matter
accessibility: label cells with the coefficient value and use a diverging accessible scale
